test-method.name: createUser_withFullPayload_returns201AndAssignsId
test-method.description: POST /users with full payload returns 201 with id assigned

test-method.name: createUser_withMinimalPayload_returns201
test-method.description: POST /users with minimal payload (name only) returns 201

test-method.name: updateUser_withValidPayload_returns200
test-method.description: PUT /users/{id} full update returns 200 with updated fields

test-method.name: deleteUser_responseBodyIsEmpty
test-method.description: DELETE /users/{id} response body is empty or {}

test-method.name: deleteUser_withValidId_returns200
test-method.description: DELETE /users/{id} with valid id returns 200

test-method.name: createUser_withValidData_returns201
test-method.description: POST /users creates a new user — data-driven across valid-users.json

param.value: UserRequest(name=Bob Smith, username=bsmith, email=bob@example.com, phone=555-0101, website=bobsmith.io, address=null, company=null)

test-method.name: createUser_withValidData_returns201
test-method.description: POST /users creates a new user — data-driven across valid-users.json

param.value: UserRequest(name=Carol Davis, username=cdavis, email=carol@example.com, phone=555-0102, website=carol.dev, address=null, company=null)

test-method.name: createUser_withValidData_returns201
test-method.description: POST /users creates a new user — data-driven across valid-users.json

param.value: UserRequest(name=Alice Johnson, username=alicej, email=alice@example.com, phone=555-0100, website=alicejohnson.dev, address=null, company=null)

test-method.name: getAllUsers_eachUserHasRequiredFields
test-method.description: GET /users — every user has required fields

test-method.name: getAllUsers_returns200WithNonEmptyList
test-method.description: GET /users returns 200 with a non-empty JSON array

test-method.name: getUserById_deserialisesToPojo
test-method.description: GET /users/{id} — response deserialises to UserResponse POJO

test-method.name: getUserById_returnsExpectedUser
test-method.description: GET /users/{id} returns correct user for id=1

test-method.name: getUserById_withNonExistentId_returns404
test-method.description: GET /users/{id} with non-existent id returns 404

test-method.name: createPost_withValidData_returns201
test-method.description: POST /posts creates a post — data-driven

param.value: PostRequest(userId=1, title=Enterprise Test Automation Patterns, body=Layered test frameworks with service abstractions make automation maintainable at scale.)

test-method.name: createPost_withValidData_returns201
test-method.description: POST /posts creates a post — data-driven

param.value: PostRequest(userId=1, title=Understanding REST API Testing, body=REST APIs are the backbone of modern software. This post covers best practices for testing them systematically.)

test-method.name: createPost_withValidData_returns201
test-method.description: POST /posts creates a post — data-driven

param.value: PostRequest(userId=2, title=Why JSON Schema Validation Matters, body=Contract testing catches API drift early, reducing production incidents and saving debugging time.)

test-method.name: deletePost_returns200
test-method.description: DELETE /posts/{id} returns 200

test-method.name: getAllPosts_returns200
test-method.description: GET /posts returns 200 with non-empty list

test-method.name: getPostById_returnsExpectedPost
test-method.description: GET /posts/{id} returns correct post for id=1

test-method.name: getPostsByUserId_returnsOnlyUserPosts
test-method.description: GET /posts?userId=1 filters posts by user

test-method.name: updatePost_returns200WithUpdatedFields
test-method.description: PUT /posts/{id} full update returns 200
